request.system: You are a regulatory analyst. Write a 2-3 sentence storyline summary (≤500 chars total) of the regulatory pressure history for this prediction-market contract. For 'retrospective' kind, write past tense and reference the actual resolution. For 'active' kind, write present tense and note what's still pending. Return JSON {text: ...}.
request.user:
Contract: Will the SEC approve a spot Solana ETF in 2025?
Kind: retrospective
Timeline (20 events):
- 2025-12-29 [background] Final Rule Delegation of Authority to the Director of the Division of Investment Management IA-6934 — Delegating authority to the DIM Director may speed SEC staff processing of ETF approvals, slightly raising chance a spot SOL ETF clears in 2025.
- 2025-12-12 [background] Administrative Proceedings: Mina N. Tadrus — An SEC enforcement proceeding involving staff could distract resources or slow rulemaking, slightly lowering odds of a year-end Solana ETF approval.
- 2025-12-05 [background] SEC Announces Agenda and Panelists for Roundtable on Financial Surveillance and Privacy — Roundtable on financial surveillance/privacy could raise SEC scrutiny of crypto surveillance-sharing, slightly reducing odds of a 2025 spot SOL ETF approval.
- 2025-12-04 [B] Self-Regulatory Organizations; Cboe BZX Exchange, Inc.; Notice of Filing and Immediate Effectiveness of a Proposed Rule Change to Amend the Franklin Crypto Index ETF, Shares of which were Approved to List and Trade on the Exchange Pursuant to BZX Rule... — Shows Cboe BZX and SEC processing crypto-ETF rule changes but is not an approval of a spot SOL ETF; small bullish signal on BZX openness.
- 2025-12-04 [background] Remarks at the Meeting of the Investor Advisory Committee — High-level SEC remarks at an Investor Advisory Committee meeting could signal agency stance on spot crypto ETFs, marginally adjusting approval odds.
- 2025-12-04 [background] The Inspector General’s Statement on the SEC’s Management and Performance Challenges, December 2025 — OIG report highlighting SEC management/performance issues may make last‑minute ETF approvals less likely by implying procedural delays, lowering 2025 approval odds.
- 2025-11-10 [background] Horizon Thread (PTY) Ltd — SEC enforcement action against a (likely crypto) firm signals a tougher regulator posture, slightly lowering chances of SEC approving a spot SOL ETF.
- 2025-09-30 [background] Poking Holes: Statement in Response to No-Action Relief for State Trust Companies Acting as Crypto Asset Custodians — SEC statement criticizing no-action relief for state trust crypto custodians raises custody/legal concerns, lowering chances of timely spot SOL ETF approval.
- 2025-09-30 [background] Cultivating Confidence: The Role of Custody in Institutional Confidence – Public Trust and Oversight; Remarks before the Digital Assets Summit Singapore — SEC remarks on custody and institutional confidence reduce custody-related objections, modestly raising odds of a spot SOL ETF approval.
- 2025-09-24 [background] Agenda, Panelists for SEC-CFTC Roundtable on Regulatory Harmonization Efforts Announced — CFTC‑SEC roundtable on regulatory harmonization signals interagency coordination that modestly raises odds of an SEC spot SOL ETF approval, but is indirect.
- 2025-09-19 [background] _Federal Register_ version — A Federal Register posting by the SEC could be the formal publication of an approval order for a spot SOL ETF, which would directly determine resolution if so.
- 2025-09-17 [background] SEC Approves Generic Listing Standards for Commodity-Based Trust Shares — SEC's approval of generic commodity-trust listing standards lowers barriers for spot crypto ETFs (including SOL), raising YES probability.
- 2025-09-17 [background] SEC Issues Policy Statement Clarifying that Mandatory Arbitration Provisions Will Not Affect Effectiveness of Registration Statements — Clarifies arbitration clauses won't block registration-statement effectiveness, lowering a legal hurdle for ETF issuers and modestly raising approval odds.
- 2025-09-05 [background] Joint Statement from the Chairman of the SEC and Acting Chairman of the CFTC — Interagency joint statement on crypto oversight may modestly affect SEC stance toward spot crypto ETFs but offers no direct approval action.
- 2025-06-16 [background] Policy Statement Concerning Agency Referrals for Potential Criminal Enforcement — Policy signals tighter criminal-enforcement coordination, modestly increasing regulatory risk for crypto issuers and slightly lowering chance of SEC approving a spot SOL ETF.
- 2025-06-12 [background] Safeguarding Advisory Client Assets Investment Management — SEC investment-management guidance on safeguarding client assets may modestly clarify crypto custody standards, slightly easing path for a spot SOL ETF approval.
- 2025-03-14 [background] Final Rule Investment Company Names; Extension of Compliance Date 33-11368, 34-102680, IC-35500 — SEC's investment-company-names final rule and compliance extension affects fund naming/compliance but does not directly change likelihood of a spot SOL ETF approval.
- 2025-03-01 [background] 2025 Examination Priorities (PDF) — SEC Division of Examinations' 2025 priorities inform enforcement focus (likely including digital assets) and provide background context but do not determine ETF approvals.
- 2025-02-04 [background] SEC Crypto Task Force Designation (PDF) — SEC creating/designating a crypto task force signals heightened scrutiny of crypto products, modestly lowering chance of a 2025 spot SOL ETF approval.
- 2024-08-22 [background] 2024 — Multi-agency CFTC/SEC comment record provides general crypto-regulatory context that may slightly influence SEC stance but doesn't directly affect ETF approval.

response.text: Throughout 2025, SEC signals were mixed - a crypto task force and enforcement priorities lowered odds, while June custody guidance and September generic commodity-trust listing standards briefly lifted them. Despite interagency coordination, custody/surveillance concerns and late-year OIG and staffing distractions slowed movement. The SEC did not approve a spot Solana ETF in 2025, and the contract resolved No.
